signals.raw:

signals.headline: Bitcoin Surges Past $75,000 on Institutional Demand | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin Surges Past $75,000 on Institutional Demand | YES | HIGH confidence**

Bitcoin has broken through the $75,000 resistance level in mid-April 2026, reaching $75,901.41 as of April 21[3]. This represents a significant technical breakout supported by record-breaking spot Bitcoin ETF inflows and corporate treasury accumulation[1].

signals.headline: Morgan Stanley Launches First Major Bank Bitcoin ETF | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Morgan Stanley Launches First Major Bank Bitcoin ETF | YES | HIGH confidence**

Morgan Stanley's MSBT spot Bitcoin ETF launched on Tuesday, April 9, generating $34 million in day-one inflows—placing it in the top 1% of all ETF launches historically[2]. This marks the first spot Bitcoin ETF from a major U.S. bank, opening a new institutional demand channel.

signals.headline: $427 Million Crypto Short Liquidation Cascade | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
$427 Million Crypto Short Liquidation Cascade | YES | HIGH confidence**

A major short squeeze occurred following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire announcement, which collapsed crude oil prices and forced $427 million in crypto short positions into liquidation over 48 hours—the largest since late February[2].

signals.headline: April 6 ETF Inflows Hit Strongest Day Since February | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
April 6 ETF Inflows Hit Strongest Day Since February | YES | HIGH confidence**

Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $471 million in net inflows on April 6, the strongest single day since late February, with BlackRock's IBIT, Fidelity's FBTC, and Ark Invest's ARKB leading absorption[2].

signals.headline: In an undated **Powell speech** (linked as April 4, 2025, but contextually later), he warned **higher tariffs** will rai
signals.raw: In an undated **Powell speech** (linked as April 4, 2025, but contextually later), he warned **higher tariffs** will raise **inflation** in coming quarters (PCE at 2.5%, core 2.8%), slow growth, with uncertain persistence, stressing anchored long-term expectations[2].
